Practice Areas

Tax Controversy & Litigation

From the first audit letter through the U.S. Tax Court and beyond, we represent individuals, businesses, and fiduciaries in every phase of a federal tax dispute.

IRS Audit Defense

Field, office, and correspondence audits. We handle IDRs, manage the examiner, protect privileged communications, and defend every position on your return. Civil fraud and egg-shell audits handled by counsel from day one.

U.S. Tax Court Litigation

Deficiency petitions, collection due process appeals, whistleblower actions. Full litigation representation through trial and decision.

IRS Appeals Representation

Protests to the Independent Office of Appeals. Settlement negotiation with hazards-of-litigation analysis and written advocacy.

Criminal Tax Defense

CID investigations, grand jury subpoenas, Title 26 charges. Discrete counsel for clients facing criminal exposure.

Trust Fund Recovery (TFRP)

Section 6672 defense for owners, officers, and check-signers. Responsible person and willfulness determinations contested.

Collection Due Process

CDP hearings on liens and levies. We stop collection action and negotiate resolution while your rights are protected.

Track Record

Outcomes That Matter

Two decades of federal tax litigation distilled into a record our clients rely on.

The Firm

Counsel When
the Stakes Are
Highest.

Sterling & Vance is a boutique tax controversy firm. Our attorneys hold advanced tax degrees and are admitted to the United States Tax Court, the federal district courts, and state bars across four jurisdictions.

We represent clients whose matters demand the protections only a lawyer can provide — attorney-client privilege, work-product doctrine, and courtroom advocacy when negotiation is not enough.

Favorable outcome rate at trial and appeals
Engagement

How We Work a Case

Three disciplined phases. Clear strategy. Privileged from the start.

1

Confidential Review

Privileged intake of notices, transcripts, and exposure. We identify every procedural posture and every defense on the table.

2

Strategy & Power of Attorney

Form 2848 filed immediately. Written engagement, defined budget, and a litigation roadmap from audit to Tax Court if needed.

3

Resolution or Trial

Negotiated settlement when it serves you. Petition filed and tried in U.S. Tax Court when it does not.

Client Outcomes

In Their Words

“
A field audit of my company turned into an egg-shell situation fast. Sterling & Vance took over, protected me, and walked it out as a no-change audit.
Business Owner
IRS Field Audit Defense
“
I was contacted by CID and thought my life was over. Their criminal tax team handled everything with absolute discretion. The case closed without charges.
Individual Client
Criminal Tax Defense
“
The IRS hit me personally with a $240k TFRP assessment for payroll taxes. They proved I was not a responsible person and the whole assessment was abated.
Corporate Officer
Trust Fund Recovery Penalty

Don't Face the IRS Alone.
Retain Counsel.

Every call is confidential and protected by attorney-client privilege from the first minute. Tell us what you received, and we'll tell you what to do next.
